— from hive to honeycomb —
Why Josema

Honey, hives, training, and seedlings — one apiary, four ways to get involved.

01 / Origin

Tsavo provenance

Every batch is traced to hives set along a single wildlife corridor in Kitui — never blended with imported bulk honey.

02 / Process

Raw & unfiltered

Cold-extracted and gravity-strained only. Nothing is heated past hive temperature, so enzymes and pollen stay intact.

03 / Craft

Hive craftsmanship

Our top-bar, Langstroth, and traditional hives are cut and built for a Kitui climate — sun, dust, and rain.

04 / Knowledge

Training & seedlings

New to beekeeping? We run hands-on training at the apiary and sell bee-forage seedlings to help your colony thrive.

Hand-inspected, frame by frame — every hive along the Kibwezi–Kitui road

Learn & Grow

New to beekeeping? We'll train you and help you plant for it.

Beekeeping Training

Hands-on courses at the apiary

Learn directly at Josema Apiaries — from hive setup and colony inspection to harvesting and honey handling. Sessions run in small groups so everyone gets real time at the hive.

  • Beginner course: hive types, safety, and first colony setup
  • Intermediate course: inspections, swarm control, and harvesting
  • On-site or group training for schools, cooperatives, and farms
Bee-Forage Seedlings

Plant what your bees actually forage on

We grow and sell seedlings for the same flowering plants our own colonies forage on — so your bees have something to work with from day one.

  • Acacia and indigenous flowering tree seedlings
  • Fast-flowering forage shrubs for new apiary sites
  • Planting guidance included with every order
Hive Selector

Not sure which hive to start with?

Answer three questions and we'll match you to the setup our own apiary would recommend.

1. How would you describe your beekeeping experience?
2. What matters most to you in a hive?
3. Where will the hive live?

Kenya Top-Bar Hive Kit

Based on your answers, a top-bar hive gives you the gentlest learning curve and the lowest cost to start.

Start over

KIBWEZI – KITUI ROAD · TSAVO EAST CORRIDOR
Our Story

A family apiary on the Kibwezi–Kitui road.

Josema Apiaries Limited sits along the Kibwezi–Kitui Road in Kitui County, inside the wildlife corridor that borders Tsavo East. Our colonies forage wild acacia, forest bloom, and seasonal wildflower — never a monoculture, which is why no two harvests taste quite the same.

Beyond honey and hives, we train new beekeepers at the apiary and grow bee-forage seedlings — because a strong local beekeeping community means better forage, healthier colonies, and better honey for everyone.

200+Managed colonies
3Harvests per year
0Imported bulk honey
